data_completeness_note: No intraday position marks stored. MFE/MAE bounded by known entry and exit prices only. Underlying price at exit estimated via delta approximation; treat as directional indicator, not precise value.

mfe_mae.note: No intraday marks. Put appreciated monotonically based on eod_exit being the only exit event. MAE is unconstrained but direction was correct throughout.

signal_notes: Quality score 4/4 with 5 confluences — highest quality trade of the session
Scanner/strategy disagreement: scanner tagged LONG (orb_breakout, trend_up, rsi=79.5), vwap_reclaim generated SHORT
DIA RSI was 79.5 (extreme overbought) — vwap_reclaim correctly faded the extension
Signal was 96 min old at entry but high quality score offset staleness
execution_notes: Entry spread 5.06% — within acceptable range for low-IV ETF option
Exit filled $0.05 above limit ($1.85 vs $1.80 limit) — positive execution
exit_management_notes: EOD forced exit at 12:30. No trailing stop or take-profit triggered during 68.6 min hold
Put appreciated from $1.60 to $1.85 (+15.6%). Whether a better exit existed intraday is unknown
Low DIA IV (14.18%) means options move slowly — EOD exit captured directional gain without runaway theta decay
assessment.verdict: Signal was correct and strong. Execution was clean. EOD exit was forced but adequate. Scanner contradiction (LONG vs SHORT) was a false warning — vwap_reclaim correctly identified an overbought reversion. No evidence of poor signal, execution, or exit.

contract.iv_flag: VERY HIGH — 83.66% IV indicates elevated premium and wide bid-ask spreads

mfe_mae.note: No intraday marks. Put fell from $3.90 to $2.865 (-26.5%). Trailing stop was the exit mechanism — put may have moved adversely from the start or had a brief favorable period before reversing.

orb_forward_performance.interpretation: COIN underlying was rising from ORB signal time onward. Negative direction-adjusted returns confirm SHORT was wrong direction. By vwap_reclaim entry ($176.91), COIN was already +$4.18 above ORB signal level and continuing higher.

signal_notes: Signal age 121.6 min at entry — significantly stale. COIN price had moved materially from signal generation point.
At signal generation COIN was in orb_breakdown/below_vwap territory (ORB signal at $172.73). By entry, COIN was at $176.91 — $4.18 higher, already reclaiming VWAP.
rvol=0.51 — sub-normal volume. Low conviction in the directional move.
Very high IV at entry (83.66%). Premium was elevated; the put was expensive relative to historical norms for COIN, raising cost of being wrong.
rsi=35.4 at scanner — near oversold, not strongly below. Marginal SHORT signal from RSI perspective.
ORB forward data confirms the underlying was rising: +1.27% in 30 min from the ORB signal price.
execution_notes: Entry spread 8.4% — wide, at the high end of acceptable threshold (10%). High IV contributed.
Entry slippage -$0.07 (1.8% of fill price) — slightly elevated but within norms.
Exit spread 3.14% — tighter than entry, suggesting better exit conditions.
Exit filled $0.045 above limit — positive execution on exit.
exit_management_notes: Trailing stop fired at 28.2 min, limiting loss to -$103.50. Without the stop, EOD exit would have occurred at ~12:30 at potentially worse prices given the upward trend.
Stop triggered when put fell to ~$2.865 from $3.90 entry (-26.5% of entry value or -$1.035).
Given wrong direction from the start, trailing stop performed its intended function.
assessment.verdict: Signal was poor. The underlying had already moved materially against the SHORT by the time the signal was actioned (2-hour-old signal, price $4.18 above breakdown level). High IV inflated the entry cost and magnified the loss when direction was wrong. Execution was acceptable given market conditions. Exit management (trailing stop) limited damage correctly.

signal.scanner_trend_contradiction: Scanner emitted LONG signal but recorded trend=down — internal scanner inconsistency

contract.days_to_expiry_flag: CRITICAL — 1-day expiry. Extreme theta decay. $7.08 OTM on a 1-day option.

intraday_mark.interpretation: Delta declined 2.5 pts over 30 min — underlying drifted below strike or theta reduced sensitivity. Directionally unfavorable.

mfe_mae.note: No intraday marks. Call declined from $4.70 to $4.20. Combination of mild underlying drift lower and theta decay on 1-day expiry.

theta_estimate.note: 1-day ATM option with 44.62% IV. Theta decay over 38 min was material relative to the option value. Exact split between delta P&L and theta unavailable without tick data.

signal_notes: Quality score 2/4 with only 1 confluence factor — minimum threshold. Weakest signal of the session.
Scanner internal contradiction: trend=down but signal=LONG and above_vwap. Scanner was not internally consistent.
rvol=0.507 — barely above 0.5, sub-normal volume. Low directional conviction.
96.6 min signal age — stale. META at $602.92 at entry; no information on where price was when signal generated.
META was in an upward-trending market day (above_vwap, orb_breakout) but scanner trend field = down, suggesting recent price action reversed.
execution_notes: Entry slippage -$0.08 (1.7% of fill price) — within normal range.
Exit filled $0.08 above limit ($4.20 vs $4.12) — positive execution on exit.
exit_management_notes: CRITICAL: 1-day expiry (2026-06-03) was selected for a 38.2 min hold. This contract expired the next day.
For a $7.08 OTM strike on a 1-day option with IV=44.62%, theta decay alone would significantly erode value over 38 min even if direction was correct.
No trailing stop triggered — the loss (-$50) remained below stop threshold. EOD exit was the only exit.

The LiquidityFilter selected this contract; the expiry selection logic chose the shortest expiry that passed delta and liquidity filters. This is a structural issue: 1-day OTM calls should require stronger signals.
assessment.verdict: Signal was poor (quality 2/4, 1 confluence, trend contradiction). Contract selection amplified risk — a 1-day expiry option was entered with a below-minimum-threshold signal. Even a mild adverse drift (-$1.11 underlying) produced a $50 loss due to combined delta and theta effects. This is the weakest trade of the session: poor signal, wrong direction, and contract expiry structurally incompatible with a ~40 min hold window.

comparative_analysis.scanner_vs_quality_disagreement: Scanner score ranked COIN and META above DIA. Quality score ranked DIA highest. DIA was the only winner. Quality score was a better predictor than scanner score in this session.
comparative_analysis.signal_age_note: COIN had the oldest signal (121.6 min). Signal age did not predict outcome for DIA/META (both ~96 min, one win one loss), but COIN's 2-hour staleness coincided with the worst outcome.
comparative_analysis.rvol_note: All three trades had sub-normal volume (rvol < 1.0). DIA had the highest rvol and was the winner. COIN/META had the lowest rvol and both lost.
comparative_analysis.iv_note: IV inversely correlated with outcome. Low-IV DIA win; high-IV COIN worst loss. High IV inflates premium, increases spread cost, and magnifies dollar losses when direction is wrong.
comparative_analysis.expiry_note: META had a 1-day expiry with a $7.08 OTM strike — the least favorable contract structure. DIA and COIN had 3-day expiry.

key_findings.finding: Quality score was a stronger outcome predictor than scanner score in this session
key_findings.evidence: DIA: quality 4/4 → win. COIN: quality 3/4 → loss. META: quality 2/4 → worst loss. Scanner ranked COIN (62) > META (60) > DIA (53), opposite of actual outcomes.

key_findings.finding: Scanner/strategy direction disagreement did not indicate a bad trade
key_findings.evidence: DIA: scanner=LONG, vwap_reclaim=SHORT. Outcome: +$25.00. The strategy correctly faded an overbought condition (RSI 79.5) that the scanner treated as a breakout.

key_findings.finding: Signal staleness of 2+ hours coincided with worst trade
key_findings.evidence: COIN signal age: 121.6 min. COIN underlying was already $4.18 above the ORB breakdown level when the order was placed. ORB forward data confirms upward momentum from signal generation time.

key_findings.finding: Very high IV (83.66%) at entry made COIN an expensive trade to be wrong in
key_findings.evidence: COIN spread cost alone was estimated $16. Entry premium $3.90 on a $170 strike. A $1.035 adverse option move (-26.5% of entry) produced -$103.50 loss — the same % move on DIA ($0.25) produced +$25.

key_findings.finding: 1-day expiry contract selection for META was incompatible with signal quality and hold window
key_findings.evidence: META260603C00610000 expired 2026-06-03 (1 day). Signal quality was 2/4 (1 confluence). Hold window was 38.2 min. Combined delta drift (-$1.11 underlying) and theta decay on a near-expiry OTM contract produced -$50.00 on a ~$1 adverse underlying move.

key_findings.finding: All three signals had sub-normal volume (rvol < 1.0)
key_findings.evidence: DIA: 0.627, COIN: 0.510, META: 0.507. Session-wide STANDBY was triggered by low volume chop in the opening scan. Even the 11:21 and 11:51 re-scans showed rvol below 1.0. Low volume conditions reduced signal reliability for all entries.

key_findings.finding: Trailing stop on COIN performed correctly
key_findings.evidence: Direction was wrong from entry. COIN kept rising. Trailing stop exited at -$103.50. Without stop, EOD exit would have occurred 10 min later at a likely worse price given the continued underlying rally.
